title: Credit Crunch: Around the World of Global Credit in 60 Minutes
summary: Global credit markets swung through war and peace, making strategic insights essential for the second half of the year. Bloomberg Intelligence’s credit strategists have their sights set on 2H, but what do their outlooks hold? In this episode of the Credit Crunch podcast, Mahesh Bhimalingam, Bloomberg Intelligence’s global head of credit strategy, hosts BI’s global credit strategy team to discuss key research, data and views from around the world. title: Emerging Market Stocks Set for Weekly Loss as AI Trade Wobbles
summary: Emerging-market stocks headed for their biggest weekly decline since early March, weighed down by a renewed technology selloff that hammered South Korean shares.

title: China Should Capitalize On This Trend
summary: China had fewer tourists than Saudi Arabia last year. To bring back visitors, the country should make the most of the Chinamaxxing movement, says Bloomberg Opinion columnist Juliana Liu. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Leverage Surge Amplifying Downside Market Moves, NewEdge CIO Dawson Says
summary: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, says the “huge surge in leverage” seen across markets “in order to chase the rally” is beginning to contribute to volatility. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Traffic Flows Through Hormuz Despite Shock Ship Attack
summary: An attack on a container vessel sailing through the Strait of Hormuz has prompted some shipowners to review exit plans, but traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday.

title: Tech Volatility Pulls Global Stocks Lower Again: Markets Wrap
summary: Technology stocks dragged global indexes lower following renewed selling in chipmakers, while a report that OpenAI could postpone plans to go public also weighed on sentiment.

title: Solar and Batteries Are Coming for Gas’s Crown in Texas Power
summary: Fossil fuels still rule the Lone Star State, but renewable energy is catching up.

title: Global Stocks Fall as Tech Volatility Weighs | Bloomberg Brief 6/26/2026
summary: US equity futures drag following a selloff in memory giants including Samsung and SK Hynix as Apple's price increase announcement stoke AI cost concerns. Oil extends decline as traffic flows without major disruption in the Strait of Hormuz following a ship attack. Priya Misra of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses the market adjustment on bets for the Fed's rate hikes. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Novak Djokovic Joins General Atlantic in His Wall Street Debut
summary: Novak Djokovic, a 24-time Grand Slam tennis champion, joined General Atlantic as the private equity firm dives deeper into sports investing. “This is the first partnership of this kind for me,” Djokovic told Bloomberg. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nigeria Draws First Tranche of $5 Billion Swap With UAE Bank
summary: Nigeria has accessed the first tranche of a $5 billion derivatives deal with United Arab Emirates’ largest lender, pressing ahead with a transaction that has been scrutinized for being opaque.

title: European Gas Prices Fluctuate as Traders Monitor Hormuz Risk
summary: European natural gas prices fluctuated as traders assess security risks after an attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz just as Qatar prepares to ramp up exports.

title: AI Is Splitting Tech Stocks Into Winners and Losers
summary: This week’s stock moves show that the AI rally has stopped being a single trade.

title: Apple Price Hikes Rattle Tech, Europe's Record Heat Wave | The Pulse 6/26/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, Goldman Sachs Head of Asset Allocation Research; Philipp Freise, KKR Co-Head of European Private Equity; Sarah Kapnick, JPMorgan Global Head of Climate Advisory (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Egypt Asset Sales Ease Path Toward $1.6 Billion in IMF Funding
summary: Egypt’s recent state-asset sales have satisfied the targets of an International Monetary Fund review, according to people familiar with the matter, easing the path to unlock about $1.6 billion in financing for an economy buffeted by the Iran war.

title: Gold Climbs Above $4,000 After Inflation Print Slows Decline
summary: Gold climbed above $4,000 an ounce on Friday after the latest US inflation print tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike, capping a tumultuous week that saw bullion slump to the lowest since November.

title: Metinvest Seeks New Investor for Italy’s Landmark Steel Project
summary: Metinvest Holding is looking for a new investor to provide equity financing for a €3 billion ($3.4 billion) steel plant in Italy as the Ukrainian group tries to downsize its commitment.

title: AI Cost Reality Check Hits Asia Tech Stocks as Apple Hikes Price
summary: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp. raised prices for their products, stoking concern that rising component costs will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

title: These Six Tankers Show Hormuz Oil Flowing Despite Setbacks
summary: The Strait of Hormuz’s reopening is quickly flooding oil markets, driving crude to erase its wartime gains. Six ships show how fuel is moving out of the key shipping lane despite setbacks like a ship attack, Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Resumes Drop as Ships Keep Crossing Hormuz Despite Attack
summary: Oil headed for the biggest weekly drop in a month after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated, although an attack on a cargo ship renewed concerns about safe passage through the vital waterway.

title: Some Fortescue Iron Ore Cargoes Held Up As CMRG Talks Stall
summary: Some Fortescue Ltd. iron ore cargoes due in China next month are being held up because negotiations between the Australian miner and the country’s state-backed buyer remain deadlocked, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Zalando Probed by German Regulator Over Disclosure Issue
summary: Zalando SE is being investigated by Germany’s financial regulator after failing to note in its annual report that Danish billionaire and biggest shareholder Anders Holch Povlsen also held a stake in a company it bought last year.

title: Asian LNG Buyers Expect Qatar to Let Force Majeure Lapse in July
summary: Asian liquefied natural gas customers of Qatar expect the supplier to let its force majeure expire in the middle of next month, as tensions in the region ease and the Persian Gulf producer ramps up exports.

title: Chinese Hedge Funds Warn the AI ‘Super Bubble’ Is Ready to Burst
summary: Two of China’s best-known hedge fund managers are warning that the artificial intelligence boom in global stock markets has become an unsustainable bubble.

title: Wall Street Embraces Dollar as Warsh’s Fed Activates Bulls
summary: The dollar is wrapping up one of its best months in a year as a raft of Wall Street banks see a turnaround of fortunes for the US currency.

title: ECB Says Consumers’ Short-Term Price Expectations Fall Sharply
summary: Inflation expectations among euro-area consumers for the next year fell significantly in May, even before a deal was reached to end the hostilities in the Middle East.

title: Johannesburg's Finances Reach Breaking Point
summary: Johannesburg halted some essential services because it can’t pay for fuel. The crisis adds to the financial woes of a metropolis of 4.8 million people, with the municipality mired in corruption scandals and unable to provide basic services. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports from Johannesburg. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Panama Canal Sees Revenue Beating Forecast After Hormuz Closure
summary: The Panama Canal expects revenue to exceed its $5.2 billion forecast for fiscal 2026 after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz drove more ships through the waterway connecting the Caribbean Sea and the Pacific Ocean.

title: Traders Pare ECB Rate Bets to Below Quarter-Point Hike in 2026
summary: Traders are reappraising the scope for the European Central Bank to lift interest rates as sliding oil prices reduce risks of sticky inflation.

title: Key Kazakh Field Cuts Oil Output as Drones Hit Russian Plant
summary: Kazakhstan’s giant Karachaganak oil and gas field cut crude production by more than a quarter after a drone attack forced the shutdown of a processing plant in Russia that handles its gas.

title: Brutal Week of Swings Leaves Korea’s AI Stock Rally in Turmoil
summary: South Korean stocks went from euphoria to anxiety in a gut-wrenching week that exposed just how fragile the AI rally has become.
